content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom C:18 visible. Let me carefully examine its connections in this detailed image.

Looking at the 3D structure, I can see atom C:18 (carbon with index 18) and need to identify which atoms are directly bonded to it.

From the image, I can clearly see:
- C:18 is connected to C:19 (carbon) - visible as a direct bond
- C:18 is connected to C:20 (carbon) - visible as a direct bond
- C:18 is connected to C:23 (carbon) - visible as a direct bond
- C:18 is connected to N:17 (nitrogen) - visible as a direct bond

All four connections are clearly visible as covalent bonds (the connecting rods between atoms). Atom C:18 appears to be a tetrahedral carbon center with four single bonds.

The geomconf_index is confirmed from the file name "geomconf_5065.xyz" as 5065.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom C:18 (atomic index 18) are:
- C:19 (carbon)
- C:20 (carbon)
- C:23 (carbon)
- N:17 (nitrogen)

These are all the atoms covalently connected to C:18.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 18, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
